item.title: Coagula Subita
item.author: estudioteh@gmail.com (Helena)
item.description:
Coagula Subita

Author: Helena(estudioteh@gmail.com)

Coagula Súbita

How would one compose silence? As someone who’s often composing quite noisy material, both in the loudness and textural sense, I’ve always found myself intrigued by quiet pieces. Cage’s 4'33" is an obvious example that comes to mind but I’m also refering to minimal ambient compositions, or whispered vocals, or synthesizers so shy they almost seem lonely.

This piece didn’t necessarily start with this objective in mind. As all things in life tend to be it was a chaotic mix of random ideas that would prop up on a day and disappear just as easy the next. Samples taken from things that were breaking in my apartment, and in my university, and on museums. It wasn’t until I started to lay out these sounds in my DAW that I heard it: The silence after listening to these often loud samples was mesmerizing! And it clicked.

Published on 2026-06-01 at algumaaaa..., last modified on 2026-06-01
